          MISSING=$(jq -r 'to_entries[] | select(.value.licenses == "UNKNOWN" or .value.licenses == null) | .key' all-licenses.json)

          if [ ! -z "$MISSING" ]; then
            echo "⚠️ Packages with missing license info:" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
            echo "$MISSING" | while read pkg; do
              echo "  - $pkg" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
            done
          else
            echo "✅ All packages have license information" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          fi

      - name: Check Frontend Licenses
        if: hashFiles('apps/frontend/package.json') != ''
        run: |
          cd apps/frontend
          npm ci

          echo "" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          echo "## Frontend Dependencies" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY

          license-checker --production \
            --onlyAllow "$ALLOWED_LICENSES" \
            --summary >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY 2>&1 || echo "⚠️ Some non-compliant licenses" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY

      - name: Check GraphQL API Licenses
        if: hashFiles('api/graphql/package.json') != ''
        run: |
          cd api/graphql
          npm ci

          echo "" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          echo "## GraphQL API Dependencies" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY

          license-checker --production \
            --onlyAllow "$ALLOWED_LICENSES" \
            --summary >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY 2>&1 || echo "⚠️ Some non-compliant licenses" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY

      - name: Generate License Report
        run: |
          cat > LICENSE_REPORT.md << 'EOF'
          # License Compliance Report

          Generated: $(date -u +"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S UTC")

          ## Summary

          - Total Dependencies: $(jq 'length' all-licenses.json)
          - Production Dependencies: $(jq 'length' prod-licenses.json)
          - Forbidden Licenses Found: ${{ steps.forbidden.outputs.forbidden_count }}
          - Production Compliant: ${{ steps.prod-check.outputs.compliant }}

          ## Allowed Licenses

          $ALLOWED_LICENSES

          ## Forbidden Licenses

          $FORBIDDEN_LICENSES

          ## Full License List

          EOF

          jq -r 'to_entries[] | "- \(.key): \(.value.licenses) (\(.value.repository // "N/A"))"' all-licenses.json >> LICENSE_REPORT.md

      - name: Upload License Reports
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: license-reports-${{ github.run_number }}
          path: |
            prod-licenses.json
            all-licenses.json
            LICENSE_REPORT.md
          retention-days: 90

      - name: Comment on PR
        if: github.event_name == 'pull_request'
        uses: actions/github-script@v7
        with:
          script: |
            const fs = require('fs');
            const summary = fs.readFileSync('prod-summary.txt', 'utf8');
            const compliant = '${{ steps.prod-check.outputs.compliant }}' === 'true';
            const forbidden = parseInt('${{ steps.forbidden.outputs.forbidden_count }}');

            const body = `## 📜 License Compliance Check

            ${compliant && forbidden === 0 ? '✅' : '❌'} **Status**: ${compliant && forbidden === 0 ? 'PASSED' : 'FAILED'}

            - Production Dependencies: ${compliant ? '✅ Compliant' : '❌ Non-compliant'}
            - Forbidden Licenses: ${forbidden === 0 ? '✅ None found' : `❌ ${forbidden} found`}

            <details>
            <summary>View Details</summary>

            \`\`\`
            ${summary}
            \`\`\`

            </details>

            [View Full Report](${context.payload.repository.html_url}/actions/runs/${context.runId})`;

            github.rest.issues.createComment({
              owner: context.repo.owner,
              repo: context.repo.repo,
              issue_number: context.issue.number,
              body: body
            });

      - name: Create Issue on Failure
        if: |
          steps.prod-check.outputs.compliant == 'false' ||
          steps.forbidden.outputs.forbidden_count > 0
        uses: actions/github-script@v7
        with:
          script: |
            github.rest.issues.create({
              owner: context.repo.owner,
              repo: context.repo.repo,
              title: '📜 License Compliance Violation Detected',
              body: `## License Compliance Issue
              
              - Production Compliant: ${{ steps.prod-check.outputs.compliant }}
              - Forbidden Licenses Found: ${{ steps.forbidden.outputs.forbidden_count }}
              
              [View Detailed Report](${context.payload.repository.html_url}/actions/runs/${context.runId})
              
              ## Action Required
              
              1. Review non-compliant dependencies
              2. Replace or remove forbidden licenses
              3. Update to compatible alternatives
              4. Consult legal team if needed`,
              labels: ['legal', 'compliance', 'license']
            });

      - name: Fail on Non-Compliance
        if: |
          steps.prod-check.outputs.compliant == 'false' ||
          steps.forbidden.outputs.forbidden_count > 0
        run: |
          echo "::error::License compliance check failed"
          exit 1


// File: c:\Users\12182\Projects\my-local-repo\.github\workflows\performance-regression.yml
name: Performance Regression Testing

on:
  workflow_dispatch:

env:
  NODE_VERSION: '18'
  LIGHTHOUSE_THRESHOLD: 90
  BUNDLE_SIZE_LIMIT_MB: 5

jobs:
  lighthouse:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: read
      pull-requests: write
      checks: write

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Setup Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: ${{ env.NODE_VERSION }}
          cache: 'npm'

      - name: Install Dependencies
        run: |
          npm ci
          cd apps/frontend && npm ci

      - name: Build Application
        run: |
          cd apps/frontend
          npm run build

      - name: Start Application
        run: |
          cd apps/frontend
          npm run preview -- --port 3000 &
          timeout 60 bash -c 'until curl -f http://localhost:3000; do sleep 2; done'

      - name: Run Lighthouse CI
        id: lighthouse
        run: |
          npm install -g @lhci/cli

          cat > lighthouserc.json << 'EOF'
          {
            "ci": {
              "collect": {
                "url": [
                  "http://localhost:3000/",
                  "http://localhost:3000/video-player.html",
                  "http://localhost:3000/dashboard.html",
                  "http://localhost:3000/social-feed.html"
                ],
                "numberOfRuns": 3,
                "settings": {
                  "preset": "desktop",
                  "throttling": {
                    "rttMs": 40,
                    "throughputKbps": 10240,
                    "cpuSlowdownMultiplier": 1
                  }
                }
              },
              "assert": {
                "assertions": {
                  "categories:performance": ["error", {"minScore": 0.9}],
                  "categories:accessibility": ["warn", {"minScore": 0.9}],
                  "categories:best-practices": ["warn", {"minScore": 0.9}],
                  "categories:seo": ["warn", {"minScore": 0.9}]
                }
              },
              "upload": {
                "target": "temporary-public-storage"
              }
            }
          }
          EOF

          lhci autorun --config=lighthouserc.json || echo "lighthouse_failed=true" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

      - name: Parse Lighthouse Results
        run: |
          echo "## Lighthouse Performance Report" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          echo "" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY

          for file in .lighthouseci/*.json; do
            if [ -f "$file" ]; then
              URL=$(jq -r '.requestedUrl' "$file")
              PERF=$(jq -r '.categories.performance.score * 100' "$file")
              A11Y=$(jq -r '.categories.accessibility.score * 100' "$file")
              BP=$(jq -r '.categories["best-practices"].score * 100' "$file")
              SEO=$(jq -r '.categories.seo.score * 100' "$file")
              FCP=$(jq -r '.audits["first-contentful-paint"].numericValue' "$file")
              LCP=$(jq -r '.audits["largest-contentful-paint"].numericValue' "$file")
              TBT=$(jq -r '.audits["total-blocking-time"].numericValue' "$file")
              CLS=$(jq -r '.audits["cumulative-layout-shift"].numericValue' "$file")
              
              echo "### $(basename $URL)" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
              echo "- 🎯 Performance: ${PERF}%" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
              echo "- ♿ Accessibility: ${A11Y}%" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
              echo "- ✅ Best Practices: ${BP}%" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
              echo "- 🔍 SEO: ${SEO}%" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
              echo "- ⚡ FCP: ${FCP}ms" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
              echo "- 📊 LCP: ${LCP}ms" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
              echo "- ⏱️ TBT: ${TBT}ms" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
              echo "- 📐 CLS: ${CLS}" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
              echo "" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
            fi
          done

      - name: Upload Lighthouse Reports
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: lighthouse-reports-${{ github.run_number }}
          path: .lighthouseci/
          retention-days: 30

  bundle-size:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: read
      pull-requests: write

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Setup Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: ${{ env.NODE_VERSION }}
          cache: 'npm'

      - name: Install Dependencies
        run: |
          npm ci
          cd apps/frontend && npm ci

      - name: Build and Analyze Bundle
        run: |
          cd apps/frontend
          npm run build

          echo "## Bundle Size Analysis" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          echo "" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY

      - name: Calculate Bundle Sizes
        id: bundle
        run: |
          cd apps/frontend/dist

          TOTAL_SIZE=$(du -sb . | awk '{print $1}')
          TOTAL_MB=$(echo "scale=2; $TOTAL_SIZE / 1024 / 1024" | bc)

          echo "total_mb=$TOTAL_MB" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

          echo "### Total Bundle Size: ${TOTAL_MB}MB" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          echo "" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY

          # Analyze JS bundles
          echo "### JavaScript Bundles" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          find assets -name "*.js" -type f -exec du -h {} \; | sort -rh | head -10 >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY

          echo "" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          echo "### CSS Bundles" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          find assets -name "*.css" -type f -exec du -h {} \; | sort -rh | head -10 >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY

          # Check against limit
          if (( $(echo "$TOTAL_MB > $BUNDLE_SIZE_LIMIT_MB" | bc -l) )); then
            echo "" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
            echo "⚠️ Bundle size exceeds ${BUNDLE_SIZE_LIMIT_MB}MB limit" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
            echo "exceeded=true" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          fi

      - name: Compare with Base Branch
        if: github.event_name == 'pull_request'
        run: |
          git fetch origin ${{ github.base_ref }}
          git checkout origin/${{ github.base_ref }}

          cd apps/frontend
          npm ci
          npm run build

          BASE_SIZE=$(du -sb dist | awk '{print $1}')
          BASE_MB=$(echo "scale=2; $BASE_SIZE / 1024 / 1024" | bc)

          git checkout ${{ github.head_ref }}
          npm run build

          CURRENT_SIZE=$(du -sb dist | awk '{print $1}')
          CURRENT_MB=$(echo "scale=2; $CURRENT_SIZE / 1024 / 1024" | bc)

          DIFF=$(echo "scale=2; $CURRENT_MB - $BASE_MB" | bc)
          PERCENT=$(echo "scale=2; ($DIFF / $BASE_MB) * 100" | bc)

          echo "" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          echo "### Bundle Size Comparison" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          echo "- Base: ${BASE_MB}MB" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          echo "- Current: ${CURRENT_MB}MB" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          echo "- Difference: ${DIFF}MB (${PERCENT}%)" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY

          if (( $(echo "$PERCENT > 10" | bc -l) )); then
            echo "⚠️ Bundle size increased by more than 10%" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          fi

      - name: Analyze Dependencies
        run: |
          cd apps/frontend
          npm install -g webpack-bundle-analyzer

          # Generate bundle analysis
          npx vite-bundle-visualizer --open=false --filename=bundle-stats.html || true

          echo "" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          echo "### Top Dependencies by Size" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY

          # List largest node_modules
          du -sh node_modules/* | sort -rh | head -10 >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY

      - name: Upload Bundle Analysis
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: bundle-analysis-${{ github.run_number }}
          path: apps/frontend/bundle-stats.html
          retention-days: 30

  api-performance:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    services:
      mongodb:
        image: mongo:7
        ports:
          - 27017:27017
        env:
          MONGO_INITDB_ROOT_USERNAME: test
          MONGO_INITDB_ROOT_PASSWORD: test

      redis:
        image: redis:7-alpine
        ports:
          - 6379:6379

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Setup Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: ${{ env.NODE_VERSION }}
          cache: 'npm'

      - name: Install Dependencies
        run: |
          npm ci
          cd api/graphql && npm ci

      - name: Start GraphQL API
        run: |
          cd api/graphql
          npm run build
          npm start &
          timeout 60 bash -c 'until curl -f http://localhost:4000/graphql; do sleep 2; done'
        env:
          MONGODB_URI: mongodb://test:test@localhost:27017/test
          REDIS_URL: redis://localhost:6379
          JWT_SECRET: test-secret

      - name: Run API Performance Tests
        run: |
          npm install -g autocannon

          echo "## API Performance Benchmarks" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          echo "" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY

          # Test GraphQL endpoint
          autocannon -c 100 -d 30 -p 10 \
            -m POST \
            -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
            -b '{"query":"{ __typename }"}' \
            http://localhost:4000/graphql \
            -j > api-perf.json

          RPS=$(jq -r '.requests.average' api-perf.json)
          LATENCY_AVG=$(jq -r '.latency.mean' api-perf.json)
          LATENCY_P99=$(jq -r '.latency.p99' api-perf.json)

          echo "### GraphQL API" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          echo "- Requests/sec: $RPS" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          echo "- Avg Latency: ${LATENCY_AVG}ms" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          echo "- P99 Latency: ${LATENCY_P99}ms" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY

          if (( $(echo "$LATENCY_P99 > 1000" | bc -l) )); then
            echo "⚠️ P99 latency exceeds 1000ms" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          fi

      - name: Database Query Performance
        run: |
          echo "" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          echo "## Database Performance" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY

          # MongoDB performance test
          cat > db-perf.js << 'EOF'
          const { MongoClient } = require('mongodb');

          async function test() {
            const client = new MongoClient('mongodb://test:test@localhost:27017', {
              authSource: 'admin'
            });
            
            await client.connect();
            const db = client.db('test');
            const collection = db.collection('perf_test');
            
            // Insert test
            const insertStart = Date.now();
            await collection.insertMany(
              Array.from({ length: 1000 }, (_, i) => ({ index: i, data: 'test' }))
            );
            const insertTime = Date.now() - insertStart;
            
            // Query test
            const queryStart = Date.now();
            await collection.find({}).limit(100).toArray();
            const queryTime = Date.now() - queryStart;
            
            // Index test
            await collection.createIndex({ index: 1 });
            const indexQueryStart = Date.now();
            await collection.find({ index: { $gte: 500 } }).toArray();
            const indexQueryTime = Date.now() - indexQueryStart;
            
            console.log(JSON.stringify({
              insert: insertTime,
              query: queryTime,
              indexQuery: indexQueryTime
            }));
            
            await collection.drop();
            await client.close();
          }

          test();
          EOF

          npm install mongodb
          node db-perf.js > db-results.json

          INSERT_TIME=$(jq -r '.insert' db-results.json)
          QUERY_TIME=$(jq -r '.query' db-results.json)
          INDEX_QUERY_TIME=$(jq -r '.indexQuery' db-results.json)

          echo "- Insert 1000 docs: ${INSERT_TIME}ms" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          echo "- Query 100 docs: ${QUERY_TIME}ms" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          echo "- Indexed query: ${INDEX_QUERY_TIME}ms" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY

      - name: Upload Performance Results
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: api-performance-${{ github.run_number }}
          path: |
            api-perf.json
            db-results.json
          retention-days: 90

  memory-leak:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Setup Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: ${{ env.NODE_VERSION }}
          cache: 'npm'

      - name: Install Dependencies
        run: |
          npm ci
          cd apps/frontend && npm ci

      - name: Run Memory Leak Detection
        run: |
          cd apps/frontend

          cat > memory-test.js << 'EOF'
          const puppeteer = require('puppeteer');

          async function detectLeaks() {
            const browser = await puppeteer.launch({ headless: true });
            const page = await browser.newPage();
            
            await page.goto('http://localhost:3000');
            
            const measurements = [];
            
            for (let i = 0; i < 10; i++) {
              await page.reload();
              await page.waitForTimeout(2000);
              
              const metrics = await page.metrics();
              measurements.push({
                iteration: i,
                jsHeapSize: metrics.JSHeapUsedSize / 1024 / 1024,
                nodes: metrics.Nodes
              });
            }
            
            await browser.close();
            
            console.log(JSON.stringify(measurements, null, 2));
            
            // Check for memory growth
            const first = measurements[0].jsHeapSize;
            const last = measurements[measurements.length - 1].jsHeapSize;
            const growth = ((last - first) / first) * 100;
            
            if (growth > 50) {
              console.error(`Memory leak detected: ${growth.toFixed(2)}% growth`);
              process.exit(1);
            }
          }

          detectLeaks();
          EOF

          npm install puppeteer
          npm run build
          npm run preview -- --port 3000 &
          timeout 60 bash -c 'until curl -f http://localhost:3000; do sleep 2; done'

          node memory-test.js > memory-results.json || true

          echo "" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          echo "## Memory Leak Detection" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY

          FIRST=$(jq -r '.[0].jsHeapSize' memory-results.json)
          LAST=$(jq -r '.[-1].jsHeapSize' memory-results.json)

          echo "- Initial Heap: ${FIRST}MB" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          echo "- Final Heap: ${LAST}MB" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY

          GROWTH=$(echo "scale=2; (($LAST - $FIRST) / $FIRST) * 100" | bc)
          echo "- Growth: ${GROWTH}%" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY

          if (( $(echo "$GROWTH > 50" | bc -l) )); then
            echo "⚠️ Potential memory leak detected" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          fi

  summary:
    needs: [lighthouse, bundle-size, api-performance, memory-leak]
    if: always()
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: read
      pull-requests: write
      issues: write

    steps:
      - name: Generate Summary
        run: |
          echo "## Performance Regression Summary" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          echo "" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          echo "### Results" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          echo "- Lighthouse: ${{ needs.lighthouse.result }}" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          echo "- Bundle Size: ${{ needs.bundle-size.result }}" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          echo "- API Performance: ${{ needs.api-performance.result }}" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          echo "- Memory Leak: ${{ needs.memory-leak.result }}" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY

      - name: Comment on PR
        if: github.event_name == 'pull_request'
        uses: actions/github-script@v7
        with:
          script: |
            const results = {
              lighthouse: '${{ needs.lighthouse.result }}',
              bundle: '${{ needs.bundle-size.result }}',
              api: '${{ needs.api-performance.result }}',
              memory: '${{ needs.memory-leak.result }}'
            };

            const emoji = (result) => result === 'success' ? '✅' : '❌';

            github.rest.issues.createComment({
              owner: context.repo.owner,
              repo: context.repo.repo,
              issue_number: context.issue.number,
              body: `## ⚡ Performance Regression Results
              
              ${emoji(results.lighthouse)} **Lighthouse**: ${results.lighthouse}
              ${emoji(results.bundle)} **Bundle Size**: ${results.bundle}
              ${emoji(results.api)} **API Performance**: ${results.api}
              ${emoji(results.memory)} **Memory Leak**: ${results.memory}
              
              [View Detailed Report](${context.payload.repository.html_url}/actions/runs/${context.runId})`
            });

      - name: Create Issue on Regression
        if: |
          needs.lighthouse.result == 'failure' ||
          needs.bundle-size.result == 'failure' ||
          needs.api-performance.result == 'failure'
        uses: actions/github-script@v7
        with:
          script: |
            github.rest.issues.create({
              owner: context.repo.owner,
              repo: context.repo.repo,
              title: '⚡ Performance Regression Detected',
              body: `## Performance Regression Alert
              
              **Commit**: ${{ github.sha }}
              **Branch**: ${{ github.ref_name }}
              
              ### Failed Checks
              - Lighthouse: ${{ needs.lighthouse.result }}
              - Bundle Size: ${{ needs.bundle-size.result }}
              - API Performance: ${{ needs.api-performance.result }}
              - Memory Leak: ${{ needs.memory-leak.result }}
              
              [View Performance Report](${context.payload.repository.html_url}/actions/runs/${context.runId})
              
              ## Action Required
              1. Review performance metrics
              2. Identify bottlenecks
              3. Optimize code/assets
              4. Re-run tests`,
              labels: ['performance', 'regression', 'user-experience']
            });


// File: c:\Users\12182\Projects\my-local-repo\.github\workflows\terraform-drift.yml
name: Terraform Drift Detection

on:
  schedule:
    - cron: '0 */6 * * *' # Every 6 hours
  workflow_dispatch:
  push:
    branches: [main]
    paths:
      - 'infrastructure/**/*.tf'
      - 'infrastructure/**/*.tfvars'

env:
  TF_VERSION: '1.6.0'
  AWS_REGION: us-east-1

jobs:
  detect-drift:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: read
      issues: write
      pull-requests: write

    strategy:
      matrix:
        environment: [dev, staging, prod]
      fail-fast: false

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Setup Terraform
        uses: hashicorp/setup-terraform@v3
        with:
          terraform_version: ${{ env.TF_VERSION }}

      - name: Configure AWS Credentials
        uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@v4
        with:
          aws-access-key-id: ${{ secrets.AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID }}
          aws-secret-access-key: ${{ secrets.AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY }}
          aws-region: ${{ env.AWS_REGION }}

      - name: Terraform Init
        id: init
        working-directory: infrastructure
        run: |
          terraform init \
            -backend-config="bucket=${{ secrets.TF_STATE_BUCKET }}" \
            -backend-config="key=${{ matrix.environment }}/terraform.tfstate" \
            -backend-config="region=${{ env.AWS_REGION }}"

      - name: Terraform Validate
        working-directory: infrastructure
        run: terraform validate

      - name: Terraform Plan
        id: plan
        working-directory: infrastructure
        continue-on-error: true
        run: |
          terraform plan \
            -var-file="environments/${{ matrix.environment }}.tfvars" \
            -detailed-exitcode \
            -out=tfplan \
            -no-color | tee plan-output.txt

          EXIT_CODE=${PIPESTATUS[0]}
          echo "exit_code=$EXIT_CODE" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

          # Exit codes: 0 = no changes, 1 = error, 2 = changes detected
          if [ $EXIT_CODE -eq 2 ]; then
            echo "drift_detected=true" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          else
            echo "drift_detected=false" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          fi

      - name: Parse Drift Changes
        if: steps.plan.outputs.drift_detected == 'true'
        working-directory: infrastructure
        run: |
          echo "## Drift Detected - ${{ matrix.environment }}" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          echo "" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY

          # Extract resource changes
          terraform show -json tfplan > tfplan.json

          ADDITIONS=$(jq '[.resource_changes[] | select(.change.actions[] == "create")] | length' tfplan.json)
          CHANGES=$(jq '[.resource_changes[] | select(.change.actions[] == "update")] | length' tfplan.json)
          DELETIONS=$(jq '[.resource_changes[] | select(.change.actions[] == "delete")] | length' tfplan.json)

          echo "### Summary" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          echo "- ➕ Additions: $ADDITIONS" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          echo "- 🔄 Changes: $CHANGES" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          echo "- ➖ Deletions: $DELETIONS" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          echo "" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY

          echo "additions=$ADDITIONS" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "changes=$CHANGES" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "deletions=$DELETIONS" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

          # List affected resources
          echo "### Affected Resources" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          jq -r '.resource_changes[] | select(.change.actions[] != "no-op") | "- \(.type).\(.name): \(.change.actions | join(", "))"' tfplan.json >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY

      - name: Analyze Drift Severity
        if: steps.plan.outputs.drift_detected == 'true'
        id: severity
        working-directory: infrastructure
        run: |
          SEVERITY="low"

          # Check for critical resource changes
          CRITICAL_RESOURCES=$(jq -r '.resource_changes[] | select(.type | test("aws_security_group|aws_iam|aws_kms|aws_rds|aws_eks")) | .type' tfplan.json | wc -l)

          if [ $CRITICAL_RESOURCES -gt 0 ]; then
            SEVERITY="high"
            echo "⚠️ Critical resources affected: $CRITICAL_RESOURCES" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          fi

          # Check for deletions
          if [ $(jq '[.resource_changes[] | select(.change.actions[] == "delete")] | length' tfplan.json) -gt 0 ]; then
            SEVERITY="high"
            echo "⚠️ Resource deletions detected" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          fi

          echo "severity=$SEVERITY" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

      - name: Check State Lock
        if: steps.plan.outputs.drift_detected == 'true'
        working-directory: infrastructure
        continue-on-error: true
        run: |
          echo "" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          echo "### State Lock Status" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY

          # Check if state is locked
          terraform force-unlock -force 2>&1 | grep -q "No lock found" && \
            echo "✅ State not locked" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY || \
            echo "⚠️ State may be locked" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY

      - name: Generate Drift Report
        if: steps.plan.outputs.drift_detected == 'true'
        working-directory: infrastructure
        run: |
          cat > drift-report-${{ matrix.environment }}.md << 'EOF'
          # Infrastructure Drift Report

          **Environment**: ${{ matrix.environment }}
          **Detected**: $(date -u +"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S UTC")
          **Severity**: ${{ steps.severity.outputs.severity }}

          ## Summary

          - Additions: ${{ steps.plan.outputs.additions }}
          - Changes: ${{ steps.plan.outputs.changes }}
          - Deletions: ${{ steps.plan.outputs.deletions }}

          ## Detailed Plan

          ```
          EOF

          cat plan-output.txt >> drift-report-${{ matrix.environment }}.md
          echo '```' >> drift-report-${{ matrix.environment }}.md

      - name: Compare with Previous State
        if: steps.plan.outputs.drift_detected == 'true'
        working-directory: infrastructure
        continue-on-error: true
        run: |
          echo "" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          echo "### State Comparison" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY

          # Get current state
          terraform state list > current-state.txt

          # Download previous state if exists
          aws s3 cp s3://${{ secrets.TF_STATE_BUCKET }}/${{ matrix.environment }}/terraform.tfstate.backup previous-state.json 2>/dev/null || echo "{}" > previous-state.json

          echo "Resources in state: $(wc -l < current-state.txt)" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY

      - name: Upload Drift Artifacts
        if: steps.plan.outputs.drift_detected == 'true'
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: drift-report-${{ matrix.environment }}-${{ github.run_number }}
          path: |
            infrastructure/tfplan
            infrastructure/tfplan.json
            infrastructure/plan-output.txt
            infrastructure/drift-report-${{ matrix.environment }}.md
          retention-days: 90

      - name: Create Drift Issue
        if: |
          steps.plan.outputs.drift_detected == 'true' &&
          steps.severity.outputs.severity == 'high'
        uses: actions/github-script@v7
        with:
          script: |
            const fs = require('fs');
            const planOutput = fs.readFileSync('infrastructure/plan-output.txt', 'utf8');
            const truncatedPlan = planOutput.length > 60000 ? planOutput.substring(0, 60000) + '\n\n... (truncated)' : planOutput;

            github.rest.issues.create({
              owner: context.repo.owner,
              repo: context.repo.repo,
              title: '🚨 Critical Infrastructure Drift Detected - ${{ matrix.environment }}',
              body: `## Infrastructure Drift Alert
              
              **Environment**: ${{ matrix.environment }}
              **Severity**: ${{ steps.severity.outputs.severity }}
              **Detected**: ${new Date().toISOString()}
              
              ### Summary
              - ➕ Additions: ${{ steps.plan.outputs.additions }}
              - 🔄 Changes: ${{ steps.plan.outputs.changes }}
              - ➖ Deletions: ${{ steps.plan.outputs.deletions }}
              
              ### Action Required
              1. Review the drift changes below
              2. Determine if changes are expected
              3. Either apply changes or update Terraform code
              4. Investigate root cause of drift
              
              <details>
              <summary>View Terraform Plan</summary>
              
              \`\`\`terraform
              ${truncatedPlan}
              \`\`\`
              
              </details>
              
              [View Full Report](${context.payload.repository.html_url}/actions/runs/${context.runId})`,
              labels: ['infrastructure', 'drift', 'terraform', '${{ matrix.environment }}', 'critical']
            });

      - name: Comment on Open PRs
        if: |
          steps.plan.outputs.drift_detected == 'true' &&
          github.event_name == 'push'
        uses: actions/github-script@v7
        continue-on-error: true
        with:
          script: |
            const { data: prs } = await github.rest.pulls.list({
              owner: context.repo.owner,
              repo: context.repo.repo,
              state: 'open',
              base: 'main'
            });

            for (const pr of prs) {
              await github.rest.issues.createComment({
                owner: context.repo.owner,
                repo: context.repo.repo,
                issue_number: pr.number,
                body: `## ⚠️ Infrastructure Drift Detected
                
                Drift detected in **${{ matrix.environment }}** environment after this PR was merged.
                
                - Changes: ${{ steps.plan.outputs.changes }}
                - Additions: ${{ steps.plan.outputs.additions }}
                - Deletions: ${{ steps.plan.outputs.deletions }}
                
                [View Drift Report](${context.payload.repository.html_url}/actions/runs/${context.runId})`
              });
            }

      - name: Send Slack Notification
        if: |
          steps.plan.outputs.drift_detected == 'true' &&
          steps.severity.outputs.severity == 'high'
        continue-on-error: true
        run: |
          curl -X POST ${{ secrets.SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL }} \
            -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
            -d '{
              "text": "🚨 Critical Infrastructure Drift Detected",
              "blocks": [
                {
                  "type": "section",
                  "text": {
                    "type": "mrkdwn",
                    "text": "*Environment:* ${{ matrix.environment }}\n*Changes:* ${{ steps.plan.outputs.changes }}\n*Additions:* ${{ steps.plan.outputs.additions }}\n*Deletions:* ${{ steps.plan.outputs.deletions }}"
                  }
                }
              ]
            }'

      - name: Fail on Critical Drift
        if: |
          steps.plan.outputs.drift_detected == 'true' &&
          steps.severity.outputs.severity == 'high' &&
          github.event_name == 'push'
        run: |
          echo "::error::Critical infrastructure drift detected in ${{ matrix.environment }}"
          exit 1

  summary:
    needs: detect-drift
    if: always()
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Generate Summary
        run: |
          echo "## Drift Detection Summary" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          echo "Completed drift detection across all environments" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY

      "args": ["servers/hootner-mcp-server.js"],
      "env": { "NODE_ENV": "development" },
      "disabled": false,
      "autoApprove": [] } } }
